title: Course: COSC 241 - Computer Systems and Digital Logic
description: Introduction to fundamental concepts of computer systems and digital logic circuit design. Covers combinational logic including Boolean algebra, DeMorgan's Theorem, logic gates, encoders, decoders, multiplexers, adders and subtractors. Addresses sequential logic such as latches, flip-flops, and counters. Includes data representation, device characteristics, and register transfer notation with emphasis on problem-solving for hardware and software design. First course in computer and assembly language programming.
prerequisites_text: COSC 112 and MATH 141 completed with grades of C or higher, or departmental permission
content:
COSC 241 - Computer Systems and Digital Logic
Credits: 3
Prerequisites: COSC 112 and MATH 141 completed with grades of C or higher, or departmental permission
Offered: Fall, Spring

Description: Introduction to fundamental concepts of computer systems and digital logic circuit design. Covers combinational logic including Boolean algebra, DeMorgan's Theorem, logic gates, encoders, decoders, multiplexers, adders and subtractors. Addresses sequential logic such as latches, flip-flops, and counters. Includes data representation, device characteristics, and register transfer notation with emphasis on problem-solving for hardware and software design. First course in computer and assembly language programming.

Curriculum role:
  - BS Computer Science: Core
  - BS Cloud Computing: Core
  - Required for CS Minor

Catalog page: https://catalog.morgan.edu/preview_course_nopop.php?catoid=26&coid=55576
